output.revisionDescription: Removed redundant class_name() wrapper from theme_block_props() call

summary.fixApplied: theme_block_props() now receives conditional array directly instead of wrapped in class_name()

before.issue: Redundant class_name() wrapper - theme_block_props() already calls class_name() internally

after.improvement: Direct array parameter - cleaner, no double processing

verification.details: Confirmed: theme_block_props() receives the conditional array as its first parameter without class_name() wrapping. The function internally calls class_name() as seen in inc/helpers/blocks.php line 74.

internalProcessing.explanation: theme_block_props() normalizes the input array and calls class_name() internally, so passing a pre-processed class_name() result is redundant and causes double-processing.

themeBlockPropsInternalBehavior.correctUsage: Pass the conditional array directly: theme_block_props(['class-name' => condition])
themeBlockPropsInternalBehavior.incorrectUsage: Do NOT wrap in class_name(): theme_block_props(class_name([...]))

baseClasses.details: All 10 blocks have the base classes applied correctly

darkMode.details: Dark mode classes applied only to blocks with darkMode=true attribute

classVerification.extraClassesNote: wp-block-takt-featured-post is automatically added by WordPress, which is expected and correct

checks.details: featured-post py-10 sm:py-16 applied to all blocks
dark bg-transparent! applied only to blocks with darkMode=true
All blocks have auto-generated IDs from theme_block_props()
10/10 images rendered with correct alt text
All CTA links present with correct href and aria-label
No broken layouts, all variations render correctly

benefits: Removed redundant function call - theme_block_props() already handles class_name() internally
Cleaner code - direct array parameter instead of nested function calls
Avoids double-processing - class_name() was being called twice (once manually, once inside theme_block_props)
Consistent with theme conventions - all other blocks pass arrays directly to theme_block_props()
Easier to read and maintain - less nesting
codeQualityImprovement.performanceImpact: Negligible but technically slightly better (eliminates one redundant function call per block render)

conventionAlignment.before: Used class_name() wrapper, inconsistent with other blocks
conventionAlignment.after: Passes array directly to theme_block_props(), consistent with theme pattern

comparisonWithOtherBlocks.pattern: All other blocks pass conditional arrays directly to theme_block_props() without class_name() wrapper

recommendations: Frontend rendering confirmed - all classes applied correctly
Code simplified by removing redundant class_name() wrapper
Now follows theme convention for theme_block_props() usage
No regressions detected - all block features still work
